title: Functions: Reusable Recipes
subtitle: Write code once, run it many times

sections.heading: What's a function?
sections.body: A **function** is a named recipe. You write the steps once. Then you can run those steps as many times as you want, with different ingredients.

sections.title: Like a recipe card
sections.body: A recipe says: 'Take eggs, mix, cook 3 minutes.' You can use the same recipe with chicken eggs, duck eggs, or quail eggs. The recipe doesn't change — only the input does. A function works the same way.

sections.caption: Define once, call many times.

sections.heading: Parameters and return values
sections.body: What you pass in (`name`) is called a **parameter**. What comes back out is the **return value**. A function without a `return` returns `undefined` — JavaScript's way of saying 'nothing was given back'.

steps.text: JS sees greet("Aria"). It looks up the function.
It runs the body, building "Hello, Aria!"
It returns that string. console.log prints it.

sections.prompt: Write a function `double` that takes a number and returns it multiplied by 2.
hints: Use the `return` keyword to send a value back.
Multiply n by 2 using the `*` operator.

questions.question: What does a function return if there's no `return` statement?
questions.explanation: JavaScript implicitly returns `undefined` when no return statement runs.

questions.question: Order these steps in how a function call works:
questions.explanation: JavaScript looks up the function, binds arguments to parameters, runs the body, then returns.
